title: astragalus lateritius
intro: Astragalus lateritius, also known as the yellow milk-vetch or yellow milkvetch, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The root of Astragalus lateritius contains compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. Astragalus lateritius is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
